meaning: n.凶手,谋杀者adj.残酷的,杀人的
examples: The industry resorted to cutthroat pricing and turned off consumers with poor service.
But at this point the competitions are hardly cutthroat, and the racers are pets first and foremost.
He served as a quiet counsel as his son jumped into and thrived in the cutthroat business world.
The deal prevented would-be cutthroat competitors from stealing market share by selling cheap cigarettes.
The odd couple even play on the same doubles team in cutthroat company ping-pong tournaments.
